entries.title: Public actions drift from viewer, authority, or runtime state
entries.symptom: A resolving viewer receives guest actions, a crawled or claimed listing is presented as owner verified, an archived, hidden, unpublished, or claim-suppressed listing leaks into public discovery, an event action ignores viewer-specific availability, or claim/review UI is enabled from preview-only or otherwise incomplete target, capability, and runtime evidence.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check design:public-surface-behavior && flutter test test/design/public_surface_behavior_test.dart && npm --prefix website run test:unit -- src/features/organizers/publicSurfaceBehavior.test.ts
guard.guardEvidence: the strict gate rejects source-enum drift, duplicate tuples, unowned routes, unverified rows, unexplained exclusions, and harness coverage gaps; the app and website harnesses execute every registered configuration against production decision and presentation helpers

entries.notes: Only Explore and its public detail/map children are guest-readable in the consumer app, but hidden tabs are not treated as access control: every consumer deep link has exactly one route class. WEB-ORGANIZER-CLAIM-TARGET-001 and WEB-ORGANIZER-REVIEW-TARGET-001 keep claim and review actions fail-closed until their canonical target evidence is live. Preview-only sync state is advisory, and review callables select an eligible canonical or compatibility target instead of allowing an ineligible canonical document to shadow a valid legacy target.

entries.title: Catch UI lint gates pass after the analyzer plugin silently skips or fails setup
entries.symptom: flutter analyze or a targeted dart analyze reports a clean tree even though the Catch plugin did not load, or a generated steering rule exists without a seeded violation.

guard.guardEvidence: fresh-plugin setup errors are fatal, all 49 plugin codes have manual or generated probes, every count is ratcheted, and every implemented code maps to a catalog owner

entries.notes: Catch plugin proof is repository-root dart analyze through the wrappers; flutter analyze remains a separate generic analyzer gate.

entries.title: Empty and error states center behind floating shell chrome
entries.symptom: A feature centers CatchEmptyState or CatchErrorState in the full box/sliver scaffold, so content appears too low behind the floating tab bar; local spacer nudges then drift between empty and error branches.

guard.guardEvidence: the manifest requires both Profile box branches, the scanner rejects raw sliver presentation composition, and primitive tests verify a 50px upward optical correction for a 100px floating obstruction

entries.notes: Use CatchStateViewport for box roots and CatchSliverStateViewport, CatchSliverEmptyState, or CatchSliverErrorState for slivers. Keep hasScrollBody true because CatchEmptyState uses LayoutBuilder for responsive overflow.

entries.title: Field-local edits regress to page-level save or permanently expanded forms
entries.symptom: A page-level Save profile button returns, multiple custom questionnaire inputs render expanded at once, or switching/cancelling a field leaks uncommitted text.

guard.guardEvidence: Host team workspace edits active professional profile inline

entries.notes: Display name, role title, bio, question-set selection, custom pack name, prompts, and options use explicit field-local commit semantics.

entries.title: Host event choices auto-expand or lose activity palette accents
entries.symptom: Entering create/edit event reveals multiple drawers immediately, allows two choice drawers to stay open, or renders activity/pace choice chips without their activity color.

guard.command: node tool/design/check_host_event_field_contracts.mjs --check && flutter test test/hosts/host_create_event_screen_test.dart --plain-name 'CreateEventScreen basics disclosures start collapsed, share one accordion, and color activity chips'
guard.guardEvidence: the scanner covers all Host call sites; the test verifies zero initial chips, mutual exclusion, per-activity pigments, and selected-activity pace accents

entries.notes: Do not use initiallyOpen for create/edit duration, activity, format, or pace choices.

entries.title: Custom field leading content and section dividers use different lanes
entries.symptom: A 48px date block or 42px avatar begins correctly but its section divider still assumes the standard 24px icon extent, leaving rules visibly short or misaligned.

guard.command: node tool/design/check_section_dividers.mjs --summary --fail-on high && flutter test test/core/widgets/catch_section_test.dart test/hosts/host_operations_screen_test.dart
guard.guardEvidence: constructor assertions and the scanner require explicit custom-leading geometry; primitive and Host Past tests require leading, text lane, divider start, and trailing section edge to agree

entries.notes: Declare leadingExtent for every CatchField.leading; custom leading content never inherits the standard icon slot implicitly.

entries.title: Host loading and guide composition collapse top rhythm or synthesize a divider
entries.symptom: Skeleton content begins four pixels below the app bar/tab rail, or a headerless first field section paints an unexplained horizontal rule directly beneath Live event guide chrome.

guard.guardEvidence: Host analytics loading uses canonical section rhythm

entries.notes: Use pageBody for normal Host tab content regardless of async state. Headerless CatchSection top rules are intentional section boundaries, not generic wrappers.

entries.title: Club editing fragments across organizer and standalone screens
entries.symptom: Club identity, contact, media, policy, or Event Success defaults acquire a second edit route or create-wizard edit mode, so capabilities, navigation, top-bar behavior, and capture contracts drift between two editors.

guard.command: flutter test --concurrency=1 test/clubs/clubs_controllers_test.dart test/hosts/host_operations_screen_test.dart test/routing/router_redirect_test.dart && node tool/run.mjs check ui:capture-coverage
guard.guardEvidence: focused tests cover owner and co-host organizer capabilities, media-only controller authorization, advanced defaults, and the legacy edit-route redirect while capture policy classifies the retired route as an organizer alias

entries.notes: The organizer Edit tab is the only canonical club editor. Keep /host/clubs/:clubId/edit only as a compatibility redirect unless product explicitly replaces the canonical surface.

entries.title: Ephemeral iOS release runners leak Apple Development certificates
entries.symptom: A clean macOS release runner has no persistent Apple Development private key, so Xcode automatic signing creates another API development certificate, the runner discards its private key, repeated releases exhaust Apple's certificate capacity, and both Consumer and Host archives fail before provisioning.

guard.guardEvidence: the app-target contract requires a reusable CI PKCS#12 source, protected secret references, non-extractable temporary-keychain import, Apple team and SHA-256 checks, a 30-day validity floor, exactly one valid development identity, and always-run signing-material cleanup while continuing to prohibit CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY overrides

entries.notes: On 2026-07-15, 17 orphaned API-created certificates and two additional development certificates without private keys or live profile relationships were revoked by exact id. The working human Development, Distribution, and Developer ID identities were preserved. Dedicated CI certificate 7P698XNLRP is intentionally retained and shared by both isolated role runners; it must not be revoked at job teardown.

entries.title: Durable workflow orchestration grows back under tool
entries.symptom: A future admin workflow adds queues, leases, retries, receipts, or agent execution to an unrelated tool subtree and recreates the current tooling sprawl.

guard.guardEvidence: boundary checker rejects legacy growth, operations imports in any new tool root, per-file orchestration markers, and split-file markers aggregated by tool subtree

entries.notes: Tools may produce bounded artifacts; operations owns durable orchestration. The v2 scanner covers all tool code while retaining file-count ratchets for known legacy producers.

entries.title: CLI shadow inventory and admin Firestore inventory diverge
entries.symptom: The local agent completes a run and exports valid stages, but no trusted path imports that exact inventory into the Firestore projection read by the admin UI, or page-sized counts are presented as full-run totals.

guard.guardEvidence: applies items before the run and replays the same artifact

entries.notes: The bridge is dry-run first, imports only immutable shadow run/work-item projections, and carries full-run aggregates independently of callable pagination.

entries.title: A process crash leaves an operations run unrecoverable or understates consumed budget
entries.symptom: A deterministic workflow clock freezes lease heartbeats, mixed or replaced guard owners break mutual exclusion, a live owner appears during stale inspection and is quarantined, a requested run-id collision poisons idempotency, an idempotency crash leaves a planned orphan, a completed checkpoint resumes with zero work-item consumption, or completion, promotion, and reconciliation lose their deterministic companion actions.

guard.guardEvidence: separate live lease clock, recovery-marker owner recheck, inode-bound mixed-owner guard recovery, run-collision cleanup, mapping-first run repair, checkpoint budget reconstruction, and completion/promotion/reconciliation action repair tests

entries.notes: Lease safety never uses --now; stale recovery installs a unique marker and rechecks directory identity plus every owner before quarantine; idempotency changes are serialized, and durable side effects are reconstructed from persisted state before replay returns success.

entries.title: Promotion eligibility changes when a live source profile changes after the run
entries.symptom: Replaying promotion for an incomplete or old run reloads mutable source policy, trusts mutated run capability/budget copies or a tampered frozen plan, accepts candidates outside durable workflow-owned selection, accepts forged companion-action provenance, changes the eligible set, or allows a blocked receipt's safety fields to be overwritten without changing its id or hash.

guard.guardEvidence: promotion requires a completed inventory-hash-bound run, validates workflow-owned candidate membership, revalidates frozen authority and companion-action provenance, and detects overwrite or replay tampering

entries.notes: The plan freezes publication policy per source; all run entrypoints bind persisted authority copies back to it, and every immutable receipt plus companion action binds canonical identity, actor, time, payload, policy hash, blocked outcome, blockers, and guardrails.

entries.title: A source rule advances from an older pass after its latest evaluation failed
entries.symptom: Canary creation searches for any historical pass, compares ISO timestamp strings instead of instants, chooses arbitrarily between equal-time evidence, or trusts a stale latestEvaluationId after a crash persisted a newer failed immutable evaluation but did not update the proposal pointer.

guard.guardEvidence: shadow canary derives the newest immutable evaluation by parsed instant, rejects ambiguous equal-time evidence, repairs a stale pointer, blocks on failure, and binds a later pass

entries.notes: Evaluation and canary operations are serialized per proposal; offset timestamps are ordered by epoch and conflicting evidence at one latest instant fails closed. The newest immutable failure repairs stale proposal state and blocks canary creation even when an older evaluation passed.

entries.title: Projection apply trusts a partial replay or a mislabeled Firebase target
entries.symptom: An existing run is reported idempotent while work items are missing or changed, the export exceeds maxWorkItems, or a production project id is passed with a non-production environment label to bypass confirmation.

guard.guardEvidence: missing-item repair, immutable-item conflict, work-budget rejection, environment-alias binding, and project-aware production confirmation tests

entries.notes: Apply is a repairable immutable import, and environment plus project must identify the same configured Firebase target.

entries.title: A counted human exception cannot be opened because it is beyond the first admin page
entries.symptom: The run summary reports a human-review count, but the read-only workspace loads only the first 200 work items and cannot retrieve or open a later active exception; run page counts may also be labeled as persisted totals.

guard.guardEvidence: controller drains the run-pinned server-filtered exception cursor, rejects summary/cardinality drift, and UI labels run and ordinary-item pages as loaded

entries.notes: The controller eagerly validates only the active exception lane, preserves a lazy ordinary cursor, prevents stale load-more writes after refresh, and keeps terminal history out of active stage queues.

entries.title: A maximum Supply Intake run exhausts the admin read window or is downloaded eagerly
entries.symptom: A run exceeds the bounded shard size, a small caller page causes thousands of exception reads, an unindexed filter combination reaches Firestore, or refresh eagerly drains ordinary inventory and breaches the callable rate window.

guard.command: npm --prefix functions run build && node --test functions/lib/operations/contractSchemas.test.js && npm --workspace catch-admin test -- --run src/features/intake/operations/controllers/useIntakeOperationsController.test.tsx
guard.guardEvidence: 1-to-10000 run capacity, committed human-review index, 200-item exception lane, two-load rate proof, maximum exception hydration, and lazy maximum ordinary-run tests

entries.notes: The callable requires authoritative run aggregates, accepts only the indexed whole-run human filter, allows 120 reads per minute, and disables automatic client retries; ordinary pages are explicit.

entries.title: A durable workflow manifest drifts from its executable surface
entries.symptom: A workflow folder, factory, command subset, stage transition, lifecycle semantic mapping, source loader, capability, or capacity changes without updating its executable inventory, or a second workflow passes metadata checks while generic runtime behavior still assumes Supply Intake vocabulary.

guard.guardEvidence: schema-backed discovery binds registry, manifest, factory methods, command subsets, stage-closed transitions, lifecycle semantics, optional source loaders, profiles, capabilities, and capacity; custom lifecycle projection tests prove runtime behavior

entries.notes: The registry and checked manifest are the extension seam for future admin workflows. Ordered stages and explicit active/published/expired semantic groups drive generic queue, export, counter, and reconciliation behavior.

entries.title: Operations runtime accepts records rejected by the canonical schemas
entries.symptom: A lightweight or hand-written validator ignores draft-07 conditionals, hardcodes Supply Intake vocabulary into the generic base contract, or allows incompatible terminal outcomes or unqueryable human-review signals into export, import, or Firestore.

guard.command: npm --prefix operations test && npm --prefix functions run build && node --test functions/lib/operations/validation.test.js
guard.guardEvidence: generic validators accept valid future workflow tokens while Supply Intake response validators reject foreign stages; Ajv also rejects missing human flags, incompatible terminal outcomes, and terminal human-review ownership

entries.notes: Canonical base schemas allow workflow-owned tokens; consumer-specific Supply Intake schemas and runtime policy narrow them at trust boundaries. The lightweight model-output validator remains limited to its declared subset.

entries.title: Reconciliation mutates or under-budgets an imported completed run
entries.symptom: Reconciliation edits the source snapshot in place, drops the source work-item capacity after through-date filtering, creates a different child for a same-day replay, accepts direct start outside the reconciliation entrypoint, or leaves terminal human-review ownership behind.

guard.guardEvidence: immutable lineage-bound child runs, source preservation, same-window replay, source-budget retention, entrypoint rejection, terminal cleanup, blocker repair, and interrupted receipt repair tests

entries.notes: A reconciliation child binds source run, plan hash, inventory hash, and UTC window and receives new work-item ids.

entries.title: Rule evaluation passes by running shipped code instead of the frozen candidate
entries.symptom: A deliberately wrong source mapping passes because evaluation ignores candidate content, duplicate outputs inflate precision or recall, unsupported code executes, or proposal identity, fixture, extractor, or candidate evidence is mutated between proposal, evaluation, and canary.

guard.guardEvidence: wrong mappings fail, allowlisted CN Traveller and Luma candidates execute, multiset metrics preserve duplicates, unknown candidates fail closed, and proposal mutations are rejected

entries.notes: Candidate families remain code-owned and non-deploying; the guard proves evaluation fidelity without claiming automatic discovery or activation.

entries.title: A completed run keeps its ids and counts while durable content drifts
entries.symptom: A deterministic work-item id is reused with changed content, a completed inventory changes without changing cardinality, a forged completion action is accepted, promotion evidence changes a previously exportable snapshot, or the same export path is overwritten with changed content.

guard.guardEvidence: content-bound if-absent writes, completed inventory hashes, deterministic completion action validation, stable post-promotion export, and immutable admin export conflict tests

entries.notes: Completed run counters and inventory hashes freeze the canonical export; post-run audit receipts are stored without changing that snapshot.

entries.title: Broad cleanup skips the deterministic audit registry loop
entries.symptom: A broad cleanup edits ad hoc files, omits registry refresh or pass proof, and leaves future agents without a durable receipt.
entries.notes: For cleanup/refactor work, also stamp a pass with focused verification proof before final handoff.

entries.title: Design-system primitives drift into aliases or feature-only buckets
entries.symptom: A design pass preserves duplicate concepts under local aliases instead of converging on a canonical global contract and Widgetbook coverage path.
entries.notes: Use canonical, repair, unify, register, discard as the review taxonomy.

entries.title: Generated design and tool manifests are stale after wide refactors
entries.symptom: CI or local checks fail after a logic-safe refactor because generated inventories, context packs, Widgetbook directories, or tool manifest entries were not refreshed.
entries.notes: When generated source changes, run the owning generator/check instead of editing generated JSON by hand.

entries.title: Widgetbook launch failures are misdiagnosed as setup failures
entries.symptom: A Widgetbook run fails because dirty-checkout compile blockers exist, but the failure is treated as a Widgetbook setup or port issue.

guard.command: Inspect the first compile error from the Widgetbook run before changing Widgetbook setup.

entries.notes: Run Widgetbook from the `widgetbook/` package and clear reported source blockers first.

entries.title: Unclaimed organizer event data crashes on non-null host fields
entries.symptom: Explore or event cards assume organizer host fields are non-null even though unclaimed organizer data allows null host ownership.
entries.notes: Model nullable fields where schema allows null and provide display fallbacks.

entries.title: Parallel Flutter tests race on native-asset build outputs
entries.symptom: Running multiple `flutter test` processes concurrently can crash Flutter while copying `build/native_assets/macos/native_assets.json` into `build/unit_test_assets/NativeAssetsManifest.json`, even when the same test passes sequentially.

guard.command: Run Flutter analyzer and Flutter tests sequentially; parallelize non-Flutter JSON/node/git scanners instead.

entries.notes: If this crash appears, rerun the failed Flutter test sequentially before treating it as product-code failure.

entries.title: Parallel subagents fragment canonical ownership
entries.symptom: Parallel agents make independent edits to canonical docs, generated registries, audit receipts, or the same source files, causing merge conflicts and multiple local truths.
entries.notes: Use disposable worktrees, disjoint write scopes, parent-owned integration, and record delegation outcomes with tool/agent/record_delegation_outcome.mjs.

entries.title: Enforcement assets drift from the rules they claim to prove
entries.symptom: A scanner, lint wrapper, manifest check, or CI step stays green while no active rule points to it, the rule has no doc anchor, the tool is only syntax/count checked, or the known-bad probe no longer proves the diagnostic fires.
entries.notes: Update rule enforcement entries, manifest role/rules/vacuityProof metadata, doc anchors, and baseline receipts in the same pass as enforcement changes.

entries.title: Explore cover promotion drops the first search result or misstates feed counts
entries.symptom: The featured event is always removed from the body even after search replaces its cover, the result label counts only visible body rows, or the map pill counts events that cannot produce map pins.

guard.command: flutter test test/explore/explore_view_model_test.dart --plain-name 'ExploreFeedSectionState counts the full feed and keeps the cover event visible'

entries.notes: Cover presentation is explicit shared state; result count uses the original full feed; mappableEventCount includes exact-coordinate internal events only.

entries.title: Explore sticky day headers escape the event stream or break sliver geometry
entries.symptom: The final day header remains pinned over recommendations or the club directory, or a pinned header nested under an incompatible sliver group throws layoutExtent exceeds paintExtent while scrolling.

guard.command: flutter test test/explore/explore_widgets_test.dart --plain-name 'sticky day headers release before trailing Explore lanes'

entries.notes: Primary Explore bounds only its pinned event slivers; compatibility callers keep pinning disabled and render inline day headers.

entries.title: Explore calendar-day fixtures change behavior near midnight
entries.symptom: A sparse-market test creates events with separate DateTime.now plus-hour calls; on a UTC runner near midnight the events land on different days and unexpectedly enable the This week strip.

guard.command: TZ=UTC flutter test test/explore/explore_widgets_test.dart --plain-name 'ExploreEventsSection renders event-first content' --concurrency=1

entries.notes: Calendar grouping fixtures must derive every event and availability calculation from one captured reference time and pin intended same-day events to one explicit calendar day.

entries.title: Explore cover falls back to chronology or promises an unavailable mutation
entries.symptom: The cover is inferred from items.first, promotes blocked/ineligible/past supply, disappears from the chronological feed, or labels a detail navigation as an immediate booking mutation.

guard.guardEvidence: ranked hero beats chronology and stays in the feed

entries.notes: Cover ranking is an independent display decision; chronological body order and actual CTA side effects remain authoritative.

entries.title: Explore social proof exposes identities or creates per-ticket reads
entries.symptom: Explore restores an unwired person card, looks up attendee profiles per ticket, or displays identifiable people without a consent-safe relationship contract.

guard.command: flutter test test/events/event_date_rail_card_test.dart --plain-name 'DateTicket exposes veiled attendee proof without identities'
guard.guardEvidence: the ticket receives only Event.signedUpCount and renders empty identity items plus veiled activity avatars

entries.notes: Identified people, mutuals, and Cross Paths require an explicit consent-safe, batched relationship source before any UI is reintroduced.

entries.title: Dark UI captures freeze halfway through nested theme animations
entries.symptom: A dark capture is written on the frame where MaterialApp finishes its theme transition, leaving CatchSurface implicit animations at a light/dark midpoint and producing false gray cards in visual-review evidence.

guard.guardEvidence: the bounded capture pump includes a post-theme animation beat and both dark artifacts visually resolve to the final dark surface tokens

entries.notes: Keep capture settling bounded because loading indicators may animate indefinitely; the extra CatchMotion.base beat targets second-order implicit animations without using pumpAndSettle.

entries.title: Explore load-more failures are rolled back and swallowed
entries.symptom: A failed cursor page leaves Explore stuck in a loading-more state, replaces the last successful page, or completes silently so the caller cannot surface or log the failure.

guard.command: flutter test test/explore/explore_discovery_window_controller_test.dart --plain-name 'loadNext restores the current page and rethrows failures'

entries.notes: Rollback only the transient isLoadingMore flag, preserve the last successful cursor window, and rethrow with the original stack so the invoking mutation or UI boundary owns presentation.

entries.title: Design parity passes only on machines with an ignored widget fingerprint cache
entries.symptom: The design-parity gate is green locally but a clean GitHub runner fails with ENOENT for artifacts/widget_dedupe/fingerprints.json, or the tracked similarity registry is compared against yesterday's ignored cache.
entries.notes: The gate extracts current fingerprints into a temporary directory, compares the tracked registry under its canonical source label, and removes the temporary input on every exit. The ignored default cache remains a local acceleration artifact, never a CI prerequisite.

entries.title: Widgetbook coverage depends on local ripgrep availability
entries.symptom: The Widgetbook coverage gate passes on a developer machine with ripgrep but crashes on a clean CI image because spawnSync returns no stdout when rg is unavailable.

guard.guardEvidence: the scanner walks tracked Dart source with Node filesystem APIs, its parser self-test rejects non-widget classes, and the full coverage inventory completes without external search binaries

entries.notes: Design enforcement must be clean-runner portable. Keep the source scan inside the Node process unless an external binary is explicitly provisioned by every supported CI environment.

entries.title: Explore silently falls back to fake geography or blank native tiles
entries.symptom: The Explore map route either renders painted fake geography or a blank native Google map canvas, masking an iOS SDK key/runtime failure.

guard.command: flutter test test/explore/explore_widgets_test.dart --plain-name 'ExploreMapScreen defaults to the native Google map' && node tool/firebase/validate_google_maps_config.mjs --env prod --platform ios

entries.notes: Production defaults to real Google tiles. Runtime proof on 2026-07-13 used an iPhone 17 simulator on iOS 26.5 and confirmed muted native tiles, custom pins, selection, the geographic distance circle, its projected Catch label, and accessible event marker descriptions. Tests, Widgetbook, and captures may explicitly select the neutral no-network pin/ring fixture; it must never paint substitute map geography.

entries.title: Consumer and Host product identity drifts across build surfaces
entries.symptom: A role builds with the wrong entrypoint, bundle/application id, Firebase registration, icon, capability, deep-link association, force-update policy, or routine release owner because target identity was duplicated across scripts and native configuration.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check platform:app-targets platform:verify-ios-release-identity platform:verify-android-release-identity

entries.notes: The gate rejects aggregate Android builds, wrapper flavor/entrypoint overrides, Apple configuration/Firebase drift, date-bearing iOS archive plist regressions, non-monotonic Apple builds, manual or explicit flavor/workflow signing overrides, development-signed exported iOS apps, a second IPA export after verification, Android AAB signer/compiled-role/Firebase/Maps/debug/package/version drift, and split Consumer/Host release ownership. Automatic signing may leave the pre-export archive development-signed; only the exported IPA is distributable proof and must have production entitlements with explicit get-task-allow=false. Its SHA-256 is checked again before that same IPA is uploaded. Xcode Cloud retirement resolves each app-scoped API workflow by its exact name (`Default`); product-prefixed CI status contexts such as `Catch | Default` and `Runner | Default` are not API workflow names. GitHub Actions is canonical; APP-TARGET-IOS-GITHUB-CUTOVER-001 and APP-TARGET-ANDROID-PLAY-001 keep external store proof honest.

entries.title: Host v2 source provenance or full-shell comparisons silently drift
entries.symptom: Host references point outside the repository, a pinned gallery has broken local dependencies, navigation chrome is masked, a focused capture is missing, or a known Today/Events mismatch grows while a whole-screen aggregate still appears acceptable.
entries.notes: App Build Matrix regenerates the four primary Host full-shell captures and runs a focused strict comparison. Stable parity debt may use a looser regression ceiling, but the canonical threshold and debt id remain visible until the feature is actually implemented.

entries.title: Marketing route metadata drifts from static output
entries.symptom: A public marketing route, page metadata key, generated listing path, sitemap entry, robots tag, or postbuild static output changes without a matching route contract update.
entries.notes: Run this guard before component-first website review so static HTML, hydrated metadata, generated organizer routes, and review states share one contract.

entries.title: Published organizer pages are metadata-only or claim readiness comes from a fixture
entries.symptom: An indexable organizer route has no semantic server-rendered profile content or JSON-LD, or a public claim CTA is enabled without a current receipt bound to the selected Firebase project and exact claim-target plan.

guard.command: node --test website/scripts/postbuild.test.mjs website/scripts/checkOrganizerBuildOutputs.test.mjs website/scripts/generateOrganizerListings.test.mjs tool/organizer_intake/sync_claim_targets_to_firestore.test.mjs && npm --workspace catch-marketing run build

entries.notes: Firestore mode must create the read-only environment receipt before website listing generation; fixture mode remains preview-only.

entries.title: Demo or waitlist-market inventory and internal organizer scores leak into public discovery
entries.symptom: Deployable organizer data contains catchDemo or waitlist-market records, Home renders past, external, or out-of-market events, or public organizer cards expose the internal profile-strength heuristic as a quality percentage.

guard.command: node --test website/scripts/generateOrganizerListings.test.mjs tool/organizer_intake/check_promotion_bridge.test.mjs && npm --workspace catch-marketing run check:event-eligibility && npm --workspace catch-marketing run check:components && npm --workspace catch-marketing run build

entries.notes: Production generation excludes catchDemo and any organizer without a live-market match by default; Storybook imports the explicit demo projection. The promotion bridge independently rejects waitlist-market organizer or legacy routes in website output. Listing strength may rank internally but must not render as public reputation evidence.

entries.title: Production marketing deploy misrepresents store-link readiness
entries.symptom: A production marketing Hosting deploy publishes fake or non-official store links, prelaunch mode accepts a nonempty store URL, or live mode accepts missing or invalid product URLs.

guard.command: node --test tool/env/check_web_hosting_env.test.mjs && node tool/run.mjs check env:check-web-hosting

entries.notes: Prelaunch requires both store URLs to be empty and renders the existing coming-soon/waitlist CTA. Live requires both official HTTPS product URLs. Deploy workflows default an absent mode to prelaunch and allow an environment-level live cutover.

entries.title: Production admin Hosting reaches an unusable Firebase Auth or App Check configuration
entries.symptom: The production admin sign-in screen loads, but phone or Google sign-in fails because the custom domain is unauthorized, the required Auth provider is disabled, or the deployed reCAPTCHA site key has no matching server-side Firebase App Check configuration.

guard.command: After production admin Auth, Hosting-domain, GitHub Environment App Check-key, or Firebase console changes, record live evidence that admin.catchdates.com and catchdates-admin.web.app are authorized Auth domains, Phone and Google providers are enabled, the deployed Firebase web app has a reCAPTCHA v3 server secret configured, and the live page completes App Check token exchange before sign-in.

entries.notes: The local Hosting env gate proves only that explicit project-aligned values exist. Console/API evidence plus a live browser/network smoke is required to prove remote Auth and App Check usability.

entries.title: Stale organizer claim decisions overwrite a newer ownership state
entries.symptom: A second active claim is accepted, or approving/rejecting an old request clears or replaces ownership established by a newer claim request.

guard.command: npm --prefix functions run build && node --test functions/lib/clubs/clubClaims.test.js functions/lib/admin/clubClaimReview.test.js

entries.notes: The club claim state owns the single active request id. Admin decisions must match it, and stale rejection may update only the old request plus its requester notification.

entries.title: Flutter, native, or notification product prose bypasses its reviewable catalog
entries.symptom: A new user-visible string is authored inline, catalog metadata is incomplete, generated native or notification copy drifts, or domain/controller state carries translated English instead of a semantic code and interpolation data.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check copy:mobile-catalog && node tool/run.mjs check copy:mobile-ownership && node tool/run.mjs check copy:native-sync && node tool/run.mjs check copy:notification-sync

entries.notes: The Flutter ownership baseline is explicit migration debt and may only shrink. The ARB and copy JSON catalogs are sources; generated Dart, InfoPlist.strings, and TypeScript are outputs.

entries.title: Admin callable contract validation silently becomes partial or stale
entries.symptom: A new admin callable bypasses request validation, a schema change is not regenerated, or a development response violates its callable response contract without naming the callable and JSON path.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check web:admin-callable-validators && npm --workspace catch-admin run test
guard.guardEvidence: generator discovers every adminApi callable and validator tests cover strict request families plus invalid response paths

entries.notes: Schema-backed contracts are strict. Callables without a dedicated JSON contract still receive a generated top-level object validator and remain visible in validation coverage until their strict contract lands.

entries.title: Storybook visual baselines cross operating-system font renderers
entries.symptom: Darwin-authored screenshots pass locally but fail broadly on Ubuntu because system-ui resolves to different fonts and rasterizers, producing text reflow and antialiasing noise that looks like hundreds of product regressions.

guard.guardEvidence: the checker pins baseline, actual, and diff paths to process.platform; the self-test rejects Darwin and Linux path collapse, while both React workflows pin blocking Linux capture to Ubuntu 24.04

entries.notes: Keep production system-ui typography unchanged. Capture and review each platform separately; never increase the pixel threshold or accept a cross-platform baseline churn to hide environment drift.

entries.title: Shared web primitives drift out of adapters, CI, or accessibility contracts
entries.symptom: A shared package export is unclassified, an adopted surface adapter stops using it, a table loses its accessible name or keyboard scroll frame, a field loses validation semantics, focus becomes invisible, or package-only changes skip both React CI workflows.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check web:shared-ui-adoption && npm run web:ui:test && npm run web:ui:typecheck
guard.guardEvidence: known-bad unclassified overlap/export and missing-adapter-import probes plus shared button, field, toggle, feedback, and table behavior tests

entries.notes: The package owns compatible native behavior and accessibility state. Website/admin adapters continue to own visual density, class names, domain tone mapping, and feature composition.

entries.title: Lazy admin screens still ship their controllers in the entry chunk
entries.symptom: A screen is rendered through React.lazy, but App.tsx statically imports its controller or adminApi, pulling Firebase Functions, sample data, and generated bridges into the production entry and exposing screen-only controls on unrelated routes.

guard.guardEvidence: Vite manifest budget plus a known-bad feature-controller import probe

entries.notes: Live route wrappers own query controllers behind the lazy boundary. Prop-driven workspaces remain independently renderable for Storybook and tests.

entries.title: Admin route stories collapse every feature and generated intake packet into one oversized chunk
entries.symptom: AdminRoutes.stories grows back toward a megabyte because all feature modules and the generated organizer-intake bridge are bundled into the story entry, slowing visual and accessibility review.

guard.guardEvidence: named feature/data chunks plus known-bad route-story and app-chunk budget probes

entries.notes: The budget covers Catch-authored chunks only. Storybook framework, axe, and other vendor runtime chunks are measured separately from application composition.

entries.title: Admin feature screens regrow into multi-thousand-line route modules
entries.symptom: A route/workspace entry accumulates private workflow panels until review, testing, and ownership become impractical, or feature-private panels are exported as an accidental shared API.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check web:admin-feature-ui-size && node tool/run.mjs check web:admin-feature-exports
guard.guardEvidence: known-bad route and private-module size fixtures plus the uppercase feature-export scanner

entries.notes: Split cohesive workflows behind lower-case feature-private module APIs. Route and workspace components remain the only public feature UI exports.

entries.title: Structural eyebrow labels collapse back into the status badge family
entries.symptom: UiLabel or AdminEyebrow is classified as catch.badge, making hierarchy metadata indistinguishable from status, count, or alert semantics.

guard.guardEvidence: exact four-stack catch.ui_label and catch.badge mappings plus a known-bad missing-symbol probe

entries.notes: catch.ui_label owns structural hierarchy context; catch.badge owns status, state, counts, and alerts. Shared typography does not make them one concept.

entries.title: Intake daily review regresses into a generated diagnostic wall
entries.symptom: Event or organizer Intake opens with pipeline diagnostics, metric walls, or raw generated cards before the operator can see the staged queue, selected evidence, readiness gates, downstream impact, notes, and backed decision actions.

guard.guardEvidence: feature tests assert both workspaces default to the staged review queue, keep diagnostics secondary, and return cleanly; primitive tests cover stage and queue selection semantics

entries.notes: New Intake stages must represent durable backend states. Discovery jobs and canonical promotion stay out of the primary stage rail until their state, authority, failure, retry, and receipt contracts exist.

entries.title: Admin route chrome regrows prototype labels and repeated title copy
entries.symptom: The admin shell shows Catch Ops, sample console, dev plus sample, or a route kicker and subtitle alongside the global route title, making prototype context look like product identity and repeating information before the task begins.

guard.guardEvidence: the component checker requires Catch Admin, a single route-owned h1 in AdminTopbar, no top-bar kicker or subtitle, and rejects known prototype labels

entries.notes: Production omits the environment badge. Development, Local, or Staging remains visible only when non-production context changes operator risk; roles and local-data context stay inside the account disclosure.

entries.title: Admin deep links are overwritten while authentication or claims resolve
entries.symptom: A production URL for a safety case, access application, organizer, event, or another role-owned record is replaced with /overview before Firebase authentication and custom claims finish resolving.

guard.guardEvidence: App-level live-mode tests preserve requested record URLs while Firebase authentication is unresolved and while a signed-in user's admin claims are pending or absent.

entries.notes: The shell may redirect an inaccessible route only after a live user has a resolved, non-empty role set. Sign-in, claim-check, and no-claim states retain the requested URL.

entries.title: Admin hook test schedules an infinite render loop after assertions pass
entries.symptom: Vitest reports every assertion as passed, then emits a React scheduler error such as window is not defined during JSDOM teardown because a renderHook callback recreates an effect dependency on every render.

guard.guardEvidence: the route-selected event test passes stable callback identities into the controller, completes all five cases, and leaves no maximum-update-depth warning or unhandled scheduler work

entries.notes: When a hook effect depends on a callback prop, renderHook tests must not create that callback inline if the effect also hydrates state. Explicit cleanup cannot repair a render loop that is already scheduled.

entries.title: Coverage instrumentation outruns the default assertion timeout at an intentional lazy boundary
entries.symptom: The full Admin coverage suite intermittently remains on Loading diagnostics after the Diagnostics action succeeds because the cold lazy diagnostics module is still being transformed when Testing Library's one-second default expires.

guard.guardEvidence: the organizer diagnostics assertion uses a five-second timeout local to the known lazy boundary; the cold full coverage suite completes 29 files and 91 tests

entries.notes: Keep the production lazy boundary and the global Testing Library timeout. Only the assertion that crosses the coverage-instrumented lazy import receives the bounded allowance.

entries.title: Visual baselines capture Storybook's preparing screen instead of the story
entries.symptom: A visual capture contains Storybook's blank preparing canvas or spinner because network-idle completed before the selected story mounted, creating a flaky or meaningless committed baseline.

guard.guardEvidence: the visual checker waits for either a visible mounted story child under a Storybook main state or an explicit Storybook error, hides the preparing screen, and forces reveal-on-scroll content into its stable visible state before capture

entries.notes: Network-idle and font readiness do not prove that a lazy Storybook module has rendered. The checker must never bless the built-in preparing UI or pre-intersection reveal state as product evidence.

entries.title: Concurrent browser captures bless broken images or corrupted render tiles
entries.symptom: A committed baseline contains a broken visible image or large black Chromium render tiles even though the same story renders correctly when captured in isolation.

guard.guardEvidence: the visual checker captures one browser page at a time and rejects visible images that have not loaded or decoded before the screenshot

entries.notes: Parallel Storybook pages can intermittently produce compositor tile corruption in headless Chromium. Visual evidence favors deterministic serialized capture over a shorter run.

entries.title: Not-found content renders underneath the fixed website header
entries.symptom: The 404 mark, title, or eyebrow overlaps the fixed Catch header because the not-found main content starts at the top of the viewport.

guard.guardEvidence: desktop and mobile not-found route baselines preserve the header-height offset and route gutter

entries.notes: Route stories must include the same PageShell class as the live app so route-specific fixed-header layout is verified instead of bypassed.

entries.title: Generic labels become unreadable inside dark Host heroes
entries.symptom: A shared UiLabel retains the light-theme ink token inside a dark Host hero, producing approximately 1.1:1 contrast.

guard.guardEvidence: Host and Host Preview shell labels inherit the dark-theme muted ink token in desktop and mobile baselines

entries.notes: Dark component shells own the semantic color override for generic labels; stories should not patch contrast with one-off inline styles.

entries.title: Native font packaging or semantic typography ownership drifts
entries.symptom: A Flutter target packages a web-only Archivo asset, app UI calls CatchFonts or owns letter spacing directly, or the generated typography contract loses family, case, alias, tracking, or variable-axis metadata.

guard.guardEvidence: finds direct CatchFonts builder calls

entries.notes: Native Flutter bundles Archivo-Roman-VF.ttf and its OFL; web surfaces may retain WOFF2. Tracking is zero by default, with named uppercase roles and the welcome reel exception owned by CatchTextStyles.

entries.title: Screens bypass canonical app-bar ownership or render under shell chrome
entries.symptom: A screen introduces a raw, helper-owned, or wrong-role app bar; a pushed route uses the correct primitive but suppresses its only back affordance; a feature Screen, Scaffold, Header, TopBar, HeaderContent, or delegated State silently owns route-title typography; a media hero is blessed by a broad allowlist; or a full-screen editor is pushed on a branch navigator and leaves persistent shell navigation covering its body.

guard.guardEvidence: the exhaustive handwritten-Dart inventory requires every Scaffold appBar, route-specific required back affordance, raw navigation bar, shell-covering route launcher, aligned root-chrome surface, and feature-owned header symbol to match a reviewed role and canonical owner; known-bad fixtures prove wrong roles, suppressed pushed-route back actions, raw Material and sliver chrome, workspace/hero escape hatches, state-delegated drift, multiple surfaces hidden behind one aligned adopter, unregistered app bars/manual headers, and branch-navigator editors fail

entries.notes: Compact edit/detail navigation remains a canonical CatchTopBar use, but pushed routes may declare leading back so the correct primitive cannot hide a navigation dead end. Root presentation is independently enforced for editors that must cover tab-shell chrome. Exact legacy manual-header classifications are migration debt and must not be widened into feature or file allowlists.

entries.title: CatchField editability and explicit-editor states drift by call site
entries.symptom: Static fields display edit affordances, editable fields lose them, direct inputs add a duplicate keyboard Tab stop, pointer-down and focus require separate taps, secondary pointers or drags leave press chrome behind, tap-away cancels before pointer-up or during a scroll, blank drawer taps or descendant keyboard events toggle the row, local Done does not close after submit, saving fields still cancel or submit or render simultaneous trailing and commit-button progress spinners, status feedback swaps without the reviewed fade/scale or leaves a dismissal timer alive, disclosure height snaps or remounts draft state, automatic reveal continues after rapid close or fights a direct user scroll, reduced motion still animates the viewport or invokes animation completion during layout, expanded commit controls remain under an overlaid shell bar, a final field cannot consume enough terminal extent to clear that bar, a populated 24 px clear target increases the canonical collapsed row height or sits off the editable value line, a disclosure chevron applies the caption reserve twice and clips into the following divider, an equal profile refresh resets a staged multi-choice draft, an active non-text disclosure leaves its field label tertiary, focus changes label typography/supporting-content order, an Optional suffix absorbs the field name instead of leaving it as a distinct text descendant, a hidden label also suppresses its same-name input hint, toggle anatomy falls off-axis or bypasses CatchToggle, choice summaries drift into feature-formatted comma lists, Optional copy is used as the final-selection policy, or empty inactive inputs and addable disclosures diverge between one- and two-line Add geometry, remount the native input, expose input-only units at rest, or recreate Add copy in feature adapters.

guard.guardEvidence: single-gesture press-to-focus/open behavior, one native keyboard focus stop per direct input, field-to-field handoff, primary-pointer press filtering, drag exit cleanup, pointer-up tap-away timing, drawer tap and keyboard propagation barriers, local-versus-controlled Done ownership, loading cancellation/submit guards, mutually exclusive saving progress ownership between a visible 13 px commit-button indicator and a fallback 16 px trailing indicator across explicit, implicit, and closed disclosure paths, tokenized status cross-fade/scale/exit with cancellable timer disposal, reduced-motion plain swaps, no chip selection layout shift, native cursor placement, onBlur delivery, persistent disclosure identity, shared single-line empty Add height and 14/600 plus Optional typography across direct input and addable disclosure constructors, same native TextField element before and after focus, input-only unit suppression at rest, combined Add semantics, equal clear/no-clear populated row height with the 24 px clear target centered on the editable value line, one caption reserve with disclosure and select chevrons centered on the 18.9 px value line and retaining canonical divider clearance, midpoint frame-driven reveal progress with at-most-8 px slide, rapid-close cancellation, direct-drag handoff, immediate reduced-motion reveal, final commit-bar clearance above a 102 px shell obstruction on a 393 by 852 profile viewport, final-Children terminal clearance without a visible end snap, Edit-only terminal-sliver ownership with Preview and Insights unchanged, equal-refresh multi-choice draft preservation, root-owned inactive ink3 to active semantic-ink label transitions across disclosure and select paths in light and dark themes, danger precedence for actual errors, tertiary Optional suffix copy while active, centered toggle anatomy through canonical CatchToggle composition, distinct optional-label text with combined semantics, retained same-name hints when labels are hidden, affordance matrix, localized empty-value fallback, midpoint expansion/collapse height, stable label metrics, explicit-save content ordering, source-option-order choice summaries joined with a middle dot, explicit body override, and clearability independent from visible Optional copy

entries.notes: New code derives affordances structurally; showChevron and the deprecated expanding/actions constructors remain source-compatible adapters to the canonical renderer. Non-text disclosure editors use CatchField.control; explicit-save text editors use CatchField.inputActions instead of nesting a second field primitive. Empty inactive editable rows share one root-localized Add value lane across direct inputs and addable disclosures. Direct inputs suppress their caption and input-only units only visually while preserving the same native TextField element; focus, value, read-only, or error restores normal geometry. CatchField resolves caption color once for all render paths: actual error, then active focus/open, then inactive tone/ink3; disabled appearance remains root opacity and Optional suffix copy remains ink3. Press chrome begins on primary pointer-down and the same gesture transfers focus or opens on pointer-up. Explicit-save tap-away waits for pointer-up, ignores drag/scroll, and cannot cancel while saving. Disclosure children remain mounted while closed. Non-centered trailing affordances share one root-owned lane after a single caption reserve and are centered within valueLineExtent; opening rotates the disclosure caret without moving its lane. Saving interaction locks remain aggregate and backward-compatible, while visual progress has exactly one capability-owned placement: a visible explicit commit bar uses its 13 px Done spinner and retains the header caret; auto-save, no-bar, or closed fields use the 16 px trailing spinner. A labeled clear target is overlaid with reserved width so its hit box cannot alter row height. CatchFieldVisibilityScope publishes obstruction only; fields reuse the nearest existing vertical scroll position and never create a second controller. Automatic reveal shares the disclosure duration and curve, advances frame by frame as scroll extent grows, yields to direct user scrolling, cancels on rapid close, jumps under reduced motion, and uses only a zero-duration final correction. CatchField.choices derives collapsed summaries in source option order with ' · ' unless an explicit body override is supplied. isOptional is visible/semantic copy only; allowEmptySelection independently governs whether the final selection may be removed. Editable empty display copy is primitive-derived and must not be recreated with feature-local plus strings.

entries.title: Software keyboards leave shell navigation covering editable content
entries.symptom: Consumer or Host navigation remains visible or is lifted above a software keyboard, the guest CTA competes with keyboard input, floating extend-body behavior remains enabled, or AppShellActiveTab publishes a nonzero bottom obstruction while viewInsets.bottom is nonzero.

guard.guardEvidence: real consumer and Host router shells hide navigation for nonzero software-keyboard viewInsets on floating iOS and anchored Android layouts, disable floating extend-body behavior where applicable, remove anchored bottom chrome, publish zero AppShellActiveTab obstruction, and preserve a focused route editor's element identity, text, and cursor selection while floating chrome disappears

entries.notes: Keyboard chrome policy is keyed to viewInsets.bottom, not FocusManager state, so hardware keyboards retain tab navigation. Consumer guest auth chrome follows the same software-keyboard suppression rule. Floating shells keep the route body in one stable stack slot and conditionally remove only the navigation sibling, preventing keyboard appearance from reparenting or disposing the focused editor.

entries.title: Production profile fields drift back to the legacy disclosure and prompt composition
entries.symptom: A field requires one tap for an outline and another for focus or text editing; a collapsed disclosure has a dead bottom-padding strip; disclosure content flickers, clips focus paint, lets nested controls trigger the header pressed state, or places validation above its drawer/action bar; Cancel/Done leaves the trailing lane or wraps into a second row; content rows reuse legacy value semantics or globally widen legacy 1/2-line clamps; multiline input typography drifts; chip, stepper, commit, or toggle controls lose exact geometry, centered hit ownership, keyboard activation, immediate focus chrome, repeat cancellation, spinner/status/error anatomy, or reviewed motion; descriptive mutually exclusive options render as terse chips with only the selected explanation detached into the field body; Photos or a headerless FieldSection loses its top rule; sibling field rows use the full-strength fieldSection tone, or replace the already-alpha-bearing line token with a darker raw opacity, instead of the muted fieldRow separator; divided and contained headers/footer metrics diverge; wrapped adapter rows lose text-lane dividers; Profile choice summaries bypass the primitive; Optional copy changes selection policy; Profile prompt cards paint adjacent outer/child vertical borders that read as a 2 px edge; or prompts collapse back into one divided explicit-save row with a modal question picker.

guard.command: flutter test --concurrency=1 test/core/catch_field_focus_semantics_test.dart test/core/catch_primitives_test.dart test/dashboard/dashboard_screen_test.dart test/hosts/host_operations_screen_test.dart test/profile/profile_widgets_test.dart test/profile/self_profile_edit_tab_state_test.dart && node tool/run.mjs check design:component-contracts && node tool/run.mjs check design:widgetbook-contract-refs
guard.guardEvidence: focused tests cover one-gesture press-to-focus/open and native cursor ownership; a tappable collapsed bottom-padding boundary; full-row sibling disclosure with descendant press isolation, unclipped focus paint, and validation after the drawer/action bar; dedicated natural-height 2/3-line content rows beside unchanged legacy 1/2 value rows; exact single- and multiline typography; wrapped choice spacing; explanatory option-card titles and descriptions inside one target; centered 32 px stepper visuals inside complete 44 px targets, pointer-up/drag-exit repeat cancellation, and acceleration cadence; a non-wrapping compact Cancel/Done row; immediate layout-neutral chip/stepper/commit focus rings; inset toggle focus chrome with one keyboard stop and one switch semantics node; exact spinner/status/error, underline, reveal, and press motion; muted fieldRow separators scale the existing light/dark line alpha while outer section rules stay full-strength; contained/divided header and footer metrics; source-order summaries and clearability independent from Optional copy; Photos and headerless section rules; direct focus-surface composition, contained first/last active rows, and production prompt question/answer focus sharing the exact section-perimeter left/right geometry; prompt question/answer composition; and dashboard content action alignment

entries.notes: The Flutter canonical primitives implement the implementation-safe tranche of handoff revision field-sys-20260714-195933-640b9906. CatchField.content maps React title/supporting-copy semantics without changing legacy Flutter body-as-value rows. Disclosure drawers are full-row siblings below the header; contained-section ordinary focus remains child-owned, while explicit section focus/error alone may change the outer perimeter. CatchFieldInsetScope independently publishes content flushing and active-overlay bleed; CatchSectionFocusSurface field-row mode owns the one-hairline overlap so both canonical and direct compositions reclaim the section's layout inset, child and section chrome paint one shared edge, and the section foreground remains the final perimeter owner. Existing semantic colors already matched and were not changed. DP-PROFILE-SELF-005 is closed for the approved primitive tranche. The direct-input empty-label portion of DP-PROFILE-SELF-006 was explicitly approved on 2026-07-16 and is now root-owned as the single-line Add presentation. DP-PROFILE-SELF-006 remains the sole owner/product/schema boundary for single-choice persistence, clearability, deselection, and save behavior; DOB helper/mono formatting; pace visibility; lifestyle footer; enum workout control; production three prompts/300 characters versus six/140; and profile icon/order changes, which must not be silently promoted.

entries.title: Section titles drift into standalone header-plus-body shells
entries.symptom: A screen composes CatchSectionHeader, caller-owned vertical spacing, and an untitled CatchSection as separate siblings, or a feature-local card rebuilds CatchSurface plus CatchSectionHeader around section-like body/actions, even though CatchSection can own the title and body contract directly.

guard.guardEvidence: SECTION-HEADER-004 rejects a standalone CatchSectionHeader followed only by vertical spacing and an untitled CatchSection; SECTION-HEADER-005 advises on feature-local Card shells that combine CatchSurface, CatchSectionHeader, and section-like body/actions. Known-bad fixtures cover both shapes.

entries.notes: The Host Organizer Manage block and HostPaymentAccountContentCard exposed the missing enforcement shapes on 2026-07-16. The scanner classifies the standalone shell as high and the broader card shell as a medium advisory without auto-rewriting title styling or trailing actions, which remain normal CatchSection constructor inputs.

entries.title: Analytics groups drift into a parallel section and row system
entries.symptom: Profile or Host analytics introduces a label-plus-gap section shell outside CatchSection; loaded and loading reports use different inter-section rhythm; Suggestions or Data coverage return to custom CatchSurface rows; backend coverage detail replaces a stable field label; or section headings lose header semantics during consolidation.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check design:section-headers && flutter test --concurrency=1 test/core/catch_primitives_test.dart test/user_analytics/user_analytics_panel_test.dart test/hosts/host_operations_screen_test.dart
guard.guardEvidence: SECTION-HEADER-003 rejects parallel dynamic kicker shells; core semantics coverage requires titled CatchSection headings; Profile report tests require zero-gap canonical sections plus CatchField content rows and stable known/unknown data-source labels; Host loaded and loading panels require canonical divided-section ownership.

CatchAnalyticsMetricGrid, CatchAnalyticsMetricTile, CatchAnalyticsDataQualityList, CatchAnalyticsBar, and CatchStatColumn remain valid display-data primitives. Profile Insights deliberately uses CatchSection.fieldRows and CatchField.content for Suggestions and Data coverage so those editorial groups inherit the same formatting contract as the rest of Profile.

entries.title: Event map becomes empty or bypasses proximity when location data is missing
entries.symptom: A nullable or partial event coordinate suppresses a pin, writes a null discovery geo cell, bypasses check-in distance, or causes a zero-radius result to replace the map and ring with a dead-end empty page.

guard.guardEvidence: keeps a coordinate-less legacy event readable until repair

entries.notes: Exact named coordinates are an event-domain invariant. Legacy reads may promote only a complete in-range pair; repair tooling never fabricates coordinates. Explore keeps the canvas and distance ring mounted for progressive radius recovery, and check-in fails closed on corrupt data.

entries.title: Canonical market ids leak into visible city copy or are replaced by labels at rest
entries.symptom: A canonical value such as in-dl-delhi-ncr is uppercased or title-cased into visible copy, or a presentation fix stores Delhi NCR where queries and projections require the canonical market id.

guard.guardEvidence: canonical market/city/alias resolution plus shared-profile and host-surface assertions that labels render while raw ids do not

entries.title: Event Detail drifts back to nested policy cards and feature-owned chrome
entries.symptom: Event Detail repeats booking facts across sections, exposes settlement copy, restores nested policy/empty-review/host cards, duplicates catch-window messaging, or uses iOS floating action chrome on Android.

entries.title: Stored cleanup snapshots claim zero while live scanners report debt
entries.symptom: A syntax-only or regex-overbroad scanner stays green, a stored snapshot says zero, and the live working tree contains cleanup findings.

guard.guardEvidence: widget cleanup ratchet plus machine diagnostic-code parser self-test

entries.notes: Zero is scanner-specific. The widget inventory ratchets live category maxima, and analyzer aggregation reads only the machine diagnostic-code field.

entries.title: Floating tab chrome obscures terminal scroll content

entries.title: Chat composer focus, geometry, semantics, and pending states drift apart
