entries.title: Event Success setup regrows forced-open legacy cards or overwrites concurrent edits
entries.symptom: The shared setup form adds feature-local card chrome, forces disclosures open, launches a second questionnaire sheet, flattens every lifecycle stage into one tool list, separates a tool from its dependent configuration, exposes a platform-owned module as a host toggle, drops platform-owned ids from an effective read or new write, rewrites a legacy stored selection during hydration, automatically applies the How people mix projection outside an explicit host edit, filters platform-owned steps out of the runtime run of show, normalizes raw text during typing, or full-overwrites a stale/frozen plan.

guard.guardEvidence: setup body uses explanatory cards for live guide choices

entries.notes: Keep selectable tools grouped by their domain stage and place rotation, reveal, and questionnaire configuration directly beneath the owning row with CatchSection.containedFieldRows. Catalog metadata is the source of truth for platform ownership: qr_check_in, safety_controls, crowd_balance, wingman_requests, contextual_openers, decomposed_feedback, and host_analytics stay hidden from hosts, are unioned into effective reads and all new draft/default/plan writes, and remain absent from untouched legacy JSON until an explicit save. Preserve every legacy selected id in the read projection. The How people mix API derives micro_pods or guided_rotations only when the host explicitly changes the composite control; never run it automatically while hydrating or serializing an existing draft. Runtime step eligibility must treat platform capabilities as enabled without shifting older run-of-show behavior. First Hello remains the sole Event Success arrival ritual.

entries.title: Runtime identifiers drift into the localization catalog
entries.symptom: A wire-map key, path/query parameter, route section value, or other lowercase identifier is migrated into ARB and later translation or copy editing silently breaks analytics lookup or navigation.

guard.command: node tool/copy/check_mobile_copy_catalog_test.mjs && node tool/copy/check_mobile_copy_catalog.mjs --check && flutter test test/hosts/host_analytics_repository_test.dart test/routing/router_redirect_test.dart
guard.guardEvidence: the copy scanner rejects identifier-shaped Visiblecopy values outside the narrow allowlist, while trend wire keys and Host route literals stay pinned by focused tests

entries.notes: Localization owns visible prose only. Runtime identifiers remain typed constants or routing literals at their contract owner; allowlist entries require an exact key and reason.

entries.title: Typed CatchField config collapses explicit unbounded multiline input
entries.symptom: A per-mode config projection uses null-coalescing for maxLines, turning an intentional null multiline contract into a one-line field and corrupting profile prompt editing behavior.

guard.guardEvidence: the field matrix pins explicit null maxLines and the profile suite pins multiline prompt rendering plus repeated-empty-line normalization

entries.notes: Null is a meaningful TextField maxLines value. Branch on config presence before reading maxLines; do not use a null-coalescing fallback on the stored value.

entries.title: Typed CatchField configs lose public widget identity or state continuity
entries.symptom: Per-mode CatchField typing regresses to Object-backed record payloads and runtime materialization, a config gains fields read only by another mode, public constructors stop being const, find.byType(CatchField) no longer finds private mode implementations, or a keyed transition between field modes replaces _CatchFieldState instead of updating it.

guard.command: flutter test --concurrency=1 test/core/widgets/catch_field && flutter analyze --no-fatal-infos lib/core/widgets/catch_field.dart lib/core/widgets/catch_field_configs.dart lib/core/widgets/catch_field_state.dart
guard.guardEvidence: the per-mode matrix compiles every public const facade against sealed private row, toggle, edit, select, and control implementations; the identity test asserts public runtimeType, Widget.canUpdate compatibility, find.byType(CatchField), and the same State object across a keyed row-to-toggle transition; focused analysis rejects an incomplete exhaustive switch or incompatible config field

entries.notes: The private mode implementations are both the widget and its sealed config, avoiding nested parameterized object construction inside const initializer lists. CatchField overrides runtimeType with the public facade type so Flutter's Widget.canUpdate contract and existing exact-type finders retain their pre-refactor behavior while Dart type tests and the exhaustive config switch still see the private implementation subtype.

entries.title: Collection-valued Riverpod family keys restart profile loading
entries.symptom: A freshly allocated List is passed to a Riverpod family whose generated provider compares arguments by identity, creating a new profile request on rebuild and leaving the host roster in a repeated loading cycle.

guard.command: flutter test --concurrency=1 test/hosts/host_create_event_screen_test.dart --plain-name 'host manage keeps ritual controls in Live and check-in in Guests' && flutter test --concurrency=1 test/event_success/event_success_manual_qa_screen_test.dart
guard.guardEvidence: the host-manage test opens the live roster, renders both profiles, and asserts the repository is fetched exactly once; the manual-QA suite proves its scoped fixture overrides the full profile family and settles after host-section rebuilds

entries.notes: Until the provider accepts a value-equality key type, stabilize the ordered uid list by contents in the owning ConsumerState before watching or invalidating the family. Scoped fixtures must override the whole family because the stable list intentionally has a different identity from the source view model list.

entries.title: Public widget contracts silently become independent design concepts
entries.symptom: A new top-level handoff contract or public wrapper increments the claimed design-system concept count even though it is only a variant, anatomy, adapter, recipe, feature composition, or screen; or a generated cluster id changes and silently disconnects a prior consolidation decision.

guard.command: node --test tool/design/component_concepts.test.mjs && node tool/design/check_component_contracts.mjs && node tool/design/check_widget_classification.mjs && node tool/design/build_widget_similarity.mjs --check && node tool/design/build_design_sync_manifest.mjs --check
guard.guardEvidence: known-bad duplicate primaries and missing parents fail; every contracted class has an explicit concept role; similarity decisions reconcile by normalized member set; generated metrics keep unclassified entries at zero

entries.notes: Count primary concept ids, not files, classes, or top-level contract entries. Preserve useful public adapters and recipes as members. Feature composition and screens remain documented but never claim a concept id. A collision is a review candidate, not a merge instruction.

entries.title: Cross-stack field vocabulary drifts from the Flutter-owned API
entries.symptom: A CatchField facade, semantic slot, save state, or CatchSection variant changes without refreshing the machine-readable vocabulary; or a React admin form claims parity without declaring and using a valid native mode mapping.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check design:field-facade-inventory web:react-component-governance web:admin-components
guard.guardEvidence: seeded deleted-facade and added-slot probes change the generated inventory, missing use-when metadata fails, source-derived modes/save states/section variants and slots must exactly match the contract, and the Organizer Publishing adoption rejects missing or invalid component mode declarations

entries.notes: Flutter owns interaction names and behavior. React implementations remain native; only the semantic vocabulary and adoption evidence are shared.

entries.title: Filtered tool checks pass without selecting any tools
entries.symptom: A renamed or invented CI category selects zero manifest tools, exits successfully, and creates a green lane that executed no checks.

guard.guardEvidence: known-bad empty selections and category omissions fail while the checked workflow declares every active manifest category

entries.notes: Filtered list and check invocations fail with usage exit 64. Impact routing additionally fails when a changed path has no root relationship.

entries.title: Widgetbook metadata passes while its real consumer no longer compiles
entries.symptom: Use-case annotations and generated directories remain structurally current while a stale widget constructor, callback return type, or generated route breaks the Widgetbook application.

guard.command: (cd widgetbook && flutter analyze --no-fatal-warnings --no-fatal-infos && flutter build web --release)
guard.guardEvidence: Flutter CI analyzes and compiles the path-dependent Widgetbook package as a web application

entries.notes: Consumer compilation complements, rather than replaces, component-contract and use-case coverage scanners.

entries.title: Repository hygiene crashes on dependency symlinks or tracked retention evidence
entries.symptom: A dry run follows a nested dependency symlink, rejects a tracked child of a mixed evidence directory, or omits a large secondary-package cache.

guard.guardEvidence: nested symlinks are counted without traversal, candidate-root symlinks are rejected, tracked evidence is preserved, and Widgetbook cache targets are reported

entries.notes: The cleaner remains dry-run by default and never deletes tracked paths or follows symlink targets.

entries.title: Legacy workflow artifacts survive without enforceable retirement metadata
entries.symptom: Supply Intake consumes a legacy artifact path that is absent from its workflow manifest, so the compatibility seam gains new orchestration or never reaches a coordinated deletion point.

guard.guardEvidence: the registry's consumed artifact patterns must exactly equal manifest compatibility inputs, each of which names a replacement owner, forbids new orchestration, and carries retirement criteria

entries.notes: Retire the producer, adapter, manifest entry, and generated artifact family together only after shadow-equivalence criteria are proved.

entries.title: App-shell integration passes only through a foregrounded desktop process
entries.symptom: Native macOS foregrounding stalls the suite, repeated app containers retain router or localization state, mounted timers make event-queue drains never finish, or consumer tests continue asserting host-only and retired Dashboard actions.

guard.command: bash tool/test_app_shell_integration.sh && flutter test --concurrency=1 test/core/catch_primitives_test.dart --plain-name 'CatchTabBar keeps four animated destinations within 390px'
guard.guardEvidence: headless wrappers run all current routed consumer journeys with fresh router/provider lifecycles and bounded clocks; the four-destination shell cannot overflow its 390px viewport

entries.notes: Native execution remains available by passing a device id. Host-only creation and attendance are owned by host surface/controller tests, while recommendations are owned by Explore.

entries.title: Route inventory assumes the router constructor is returned inline
entries.symptom: Giving GoRouter an explicit lifecycle owner requires assigning the constructor before return and routing child pages through keys.root or a helper's rootNavigatorKey parameter, while source scanners or chrome-policy tests still recognize only the former inline constructor and _rootNavigatorKey spelling.

guard.guardEvidence: known-good inline-return and lifecycle-owned constructor fixtures both parse, the source-derived route inventory remains current, and every shell child route targets the lifecycle-owned root navigator key directly or through the typed helper parameter

entries.notes: The scanner accepts only a locally owned final or var assignment or an inline return; it still fails closed when no GoRouter configuration can be found. Source-policy assertions must follow the lifecycle-owned keys.root contract and its rootNavigatorKey helper parameter instead of restoring the removed global key.

entries.title: Reviewed macOS goldens fail on hosted-runner antialiasing noise
entries.symptom: The pinned hosted macOS runner changes a small number of anti-aliased text and icon edge pixels even though layout, color, content, and component geometry are unchanged, causing the exact comparator to skip the app-shell integration stage.

guard.guardEvidence: CatchGoldenFileComparator allows at most 0.30% differing pixels; its focused test proves 0.25% passes and 0.50% fails, and CI retains native Flutter diff artifacts above the threshold

entries.notes: The 0.30% ceiling is grounded in PR #94 run 29701230738, where hosted-macOS differences peaked at 0.29% and visual inspection localized them to anti-aliased edges.

entries.title: Agent readiness passes only after local Functions build residue exists
entries.symptom: A guard builds Functions and then runs compiled functions/lib tests, but readiness checks the ignored build output itself, so a dirty developer checkout passes while a clean CI checkout fails.

guard.guardEvidence: focused extraction tests prove declared Functions build outputs resolve to tracked TypeScript sources while an undeclared generated path still fails closed

entries.notes: Guard commands remain executable against compiled JavaScript; only readiness's static path proof maps build outputs back to their tracked source contract.

entries.title: Flutter field inventory is stale after an unrelated product-source edit
entries.symptom: The field-surface inventory hashes route and screen contracts and records source line anchors, but impacted checks omit its generator after a product-source edit, so local impacted verification passes and the full design CI bucket later reports stale evidence.

guard.guardEvidence: the impact-routing fixture proves a Flutter product path selects design:flutter-field-surface-inventory, and the generator verifies current source hashes and line anchors

entries.notes: The design-system relationship already covers lib consumers; this regression binds the field inventory gate to that relationship instead of relying on agents to remember a hidden transitive hash dependency.

entries.title: Tab-root scroll contracts assume file-global shell navigator keys
entries.symptom: Router navigator identity moves into a lifecycle-owned key object, but the tab-root scanner parses only a bare identifier and its manifest retains retired file-global key names, so every real shell branch is simultaneously reported missing and unregistered.

guard.guardEvidence: the focused lifecycle-owned member-access fixture passes while the existing unregistered-branch fixture still proves new branches fail closed

entries.notes: The manifest records the typed lifecycle expression used at each StatefulShellBranch callsite, such as keys.dashboard, instead of restoring disposed-container-sensitive globals.

entries.title: Admin bundle budget passes only when a local dist artifact already exists
entries.symptom: The test tool category invokes the admin bundle ratchet against ignored dist output without first producing a release build, so developer residue passes while a clean CI checkout fails on a missing Vite manifest.

guard.guardEvidence: the production build writes dist/.vite/manifest.json and immediately runs the bundle-budget ratchet through the admin build script

entries.notes: The manifest classifies this gate as build-artifact-producing and invokes the owning build instead of allowing local dist state to become an implicit prerequisite.

entries.title: Website Storybook visuals pass only when local static output already exists
entries.symptom: The website visual gate reads website/storybook-static/index.json without invoking the owning Storybook build, or runs in a browserless CI bucket, so local generated output and a machine-installed browser mask clean-checkout failures before screenshot comparison completes.

guard.command: npm --workspace catch-marketing run build:storybook && node tool/web/check_storybook_visuals.mjs --surface website --check
guard.guardEvidence: the design-category gate runs in the Playwright-prepared marketing-design CI bucket; the marketing Storybook build produces the indexed static bundle immediately consumed by all 208 current-platform visual comparisons

entries.notes: The manifest classifies the visual gate as build-artifact-producing and routes it through the browser-prepared design category; screenshot comparison depends on neither an ignored Storybook directory nor an undeclared machine browser.

entries.title: Club compatibility names silently become product authority again
entries.symptom: A new client, contract, route, media path, callable, or generic product label uses club as the parent entity even though club is only an organizer subtype, recreating split authority after the migration.

guard.guardEvidence: the seeded legacy callable and generic club-copy fixture fails while the canonical cross-surface fixture and current repository pass

entries.notes: Legacy club collections, fields, Dart aliases, and callable wrappers remain only for the released-client compatibility window; remote backfill and retirement have separate evidence gates.

entries.title: Follower backfill overwrites the legacy membership aggregate
entries.symptom: Creating organizer follow edges asynchronously rewrites clubs.memberCount from followerCount, dropping owner and manager membership edges and causing delayed post-migration parity drift.

guard.guardEvidence: the organizer follower trigger test proves legacy memberCount is unchanged, while the migration test derives an explicit recovery patch from active clubMemberships and excludes memberCount from canonical organizer parity

entries.notes: The two aggregates intentionally have different semantics during compatibility: organizer followers exclude team roles, while legacy club memberships include them.

entries.title: Initial provider loads remain indefinite or discard recovery callbacks
entries.symptom: A screen can display a skeleton forever, a known error loses to a loading flag, or an explicit retry callback disappears because generic exception metadata classifies the error as non-retryable.

guard.command: bash tool/check_catch_ui_lints.sh && flutter test test/core/catch_async_state_test.dart test/core/catch_primitives_test.dart test/explore/explore_view_model_test.dart
guard.guardEvidence: the analyzer rejects CatchAsyncValue surfaces without onRetry; adapter tests prove refresh-time errors outrank simultaneous loading flags; primitive tests prove the initial-load deadline and explicit recovery callback; Explore state tests prove known errors outrank loading

entries.notes: The 12-second policy is a presentation deadline for initial provider resolution, not an idle timeout for healthy Firestore streams.

entries.title: Pushed route titles, surfaces, and dividers drift by caller
entries.symptom: One route has a permanent underline, another never separates scrolled content, a detail bar changes surface color, or loading/content branches render different title hierarchies.

guard.command: node tool/run.mjs check design:screen-top-bar-contracts && flutter test test/core/catch_top_bar_test.dart test/events/saved_events_screen_test.dart test/chats/chat_screen_test.dart
guard.guardEvidence: the route shell test proves the divider follows scroll depth, the contract gate inventories Consumer and Host roots, and focused tests reject duplicate Saved Events titles and drifting chat chrome

entries.notes: Root tab titles stay in scroll content; pushed routes use CatchRouteScaffold.

entries.title: Blocking error surfaces strand the user without a next action
entries.symptom: A full-screen or sliver error replaces the route content but exposes neither a safe retry nor a way to leave the dead end.

guard.guardEvidence: catch_error_state_requires_action rejects actionless blocking error primitives and widget tests prove the canonical alternate action is preserved by every placement adapter

entries.notes: Use retry for rerunnable failures and CatchErrorBackAction or another explicit destination for terminal route conditions. Inline errors may inherit nearby recovery controls.

entries.title: Legacy organizer read fallback returns after canonical cutover
entries.symptom: Current clients silently read clubs after a canonical organizer error, masking rollout defects and restoring two competing read authorities.

guard.guardEvidence: the nomenclature gate rejects legacy fallback seams and repository tests prove legacy-only documents are ignored

entries.notes: The temporary permission-denied fallback was retired on 2026-07-22 after production rules, indexes, and 41-of-41 organizer parity were verified. Legacy writes remain only for released-client compatibility until a separate cleanup.

entries.title: First-party organizers disappear from verified web discovery
entries.symptom: Splitting first-party and owner-verified trust labels causes Catch-created organizers to fall out of verified filters and recommendations, leaving a registry-ready Storybook surface blank.

guard.command: npm --workspace catch-marketing run test:unit -- --run src/features/organizers/selectors.test.ts && node tool/web/check_storybook_visuals.mjs --surface website --component listing_recommended_organizers_section --check
guard.guardEvidence: the selector test proves both first-party and owner-verified organizers remain eligible while source-backed organizers do not; the visual contract proves the recommendation surface mounts with the distinct Catch trust badge

entries.notes: Verified discovery is a product eligibility grouping, not a request to collapse first-party and owner-verified presentation labels.

entries.title: Production marketing deploy checks live organizer readiness against receipt-free generated output
entries.symptom: PR validation passes, but the main-branch Hosting deploy fails because the live Firestore readiness receipt changes organizer claim and review capabilities before the predeploy drift check runs.

guard.guardEvidence: the workflow contract requires receipt-aware production and demo projections to be materialized after the read-only production receipt and before Firebase predeploy; generator tests prove a verified canonical receipt enables the intended public claim and review capabilities

entries.notes: The materialized files exist only in the deployment runner checkout. Committed generated projections stay receipt-free so pull-request drift validation remains deterministic and does not claim live production readiness.

entries.title: Feature orchestration claims complete evidence while surfaces, states, or actions drift
entries.symptom: A feature brief appears deterministic but omits an entire registered product surface, conflates Flutter and React runtime ownership, splits a filtered projection from the same user goal, groups a stateful secondary route without compiling its exact inventory, duplicates or omits an authority state, treats an implemented but unreachable state as live product flow, fabricates an action from repository capability that the route never exposes, binds a real action to the wrong but valid route authority, collapses dependency or identity loading/failure into a successful fallback, labels guidance as a completed external handoff, ignores external false/error/pending outcomes, cites an unrelated schema for a remote action whose request/response boundary is uncontracted, leaves an action or owner unclassified, points to missing evidence, falsely claims a surface binding, enables an action whose controller exists but whose route callback is unwired, models a pending request as guarded while request-defining fields, auth, navigation, or another mutation domain remain live, or carries a stale generated projection.

guard.command: node --test tool/design/check_feature_coverage.test.mjs tool/design/build_feature_contracts.test.mjs && node tool/design/check_feature_coverage.mjs --check && node tool/design/build_feature_contracts.mjs --check && node tool/design/check_screen_contracts.mjs --check
guard.guardEvidence: known-bad coverage tests reject unclassified registry additions, duplicate or unknown decisions, invalid grouping, false multi-runtime contract bindings, and orphaned contracts; compiler tests prove compact state matrices retain exact authority coverage, accept stable annotated Flutter preview-builder ids and top-level Dart function owners, reject out-of-inventory overrides, validate explicit React route-state preview mappings, preserve known implementation gaps with stable debt while rejecting any enabled known-gap action, support shared multi-runtime and read-only features, and reject runtime/authority mismatches, duplicate or missing states, missing evidence, stale exceptions, invalid outcomes, and unknown action owners/actions/dimensions

entries.notes: The reference set now includes all 32 Flutter screen authorities through Event Detail with its exact-location projection, Event Recap, Explore, Dashboard Home, Event Success, Event Planning, Matching Preferences, Host Home, Host Organizers, Host Organizer Create, Host Event Create, Host Event Manage with its owner-edit projection, Host Inbox, Catches Hub, Catches Event, Matches List, Chat Thread, Self Profile, Public Profile, Organizer Detail, Phone Authentication, Member Onboarding with its Start Welcome projection, Notifications, Reviews, Payments, and Account Settings, plus every stateful marketing route through Marketing Home, Host Acquisition, Organizer Claim, Organizer Search, and Organizer Detail. Explore is the first two-runtime feature; Organizer Detail is the first three-surface feature; Host Organizers proves that one coherent user workspace can retain separate action owners without fake fragmentation; Host Event Create and Host Organizer Create prove that meaningful decisions and effects should be cardinalized while raw field-entry events remain form data; Chat Thread proves that consumer and host policy projections of the same production implementation belong in one contract while retaining exact surface-specific states, actions, and evidence; Start Welcome proves that two registered entry points using the same implementation for the same goal should reuse one feature identity and semantic action ids; Event Planning proves that a filtered route using a different screen class can still be a projection when data, actions, and user goal overlap, and that repository capability must not fabricate UI actions. Event Location Map proves that a registered read-only child route can extend its parent feature while an unrelated fullscreen LocationPickerScreen modal must remain a parent-owned side effect instead of borrowing that route authority. Event Recap, Reviews, and Payments prove that loading, failure, and genuine absence in a secondary dependency must not be mislabeled as one successful fallback; Notifications applies the same rule to identity failure versus sign-out. Payments proves a visible CTA must be named for its actual guidance outcome rather than an unimplemented support handoff. Account Settings proves that per-controller pending guards do not imply safe whole-surface concurrency when other mutation and route domains remain active. Organizer Claim proves a dynamic route with its own state vocabulary should compile as a second projection instead of remaining a loose group. Marketing Home and Host Acquisition prove that an action contract must leave `dataContracts` empty when the actual HTTP request/response seam has no schema; a nearby projection schema is not substitute evidence. Compact state matrices reduce repeated source boilerplate while keeping the exact authority inventory explicit. Event Success demonstrates that a controller method is not sufficient action proof: the unwired compatibility callback is a disabled known gap until route-level wiring and a persistence test exist. Host Organizer Create, Host Event Edit, Phone Authentication, Member Onboarding, Matching Preferences, Reviews, Account Settings, Marketing Home, Host Acquisition, and Organizer Claim expose pending-mutation snapshot or concurrency drift explicitly: disabling a primary button is not equivalent to freezing every action that can change, dismiss, invalidate, or overlap the request, and an input callback must not reset the in-flight guard. Member Onboarding also proves that an implemented, captured state is not live-flow coverage when no production transition reaches it; reachability must be explicit until it is retired or reconnected. Matching Preferences additionally shows that an explicit missing-data state can still reveal unacceptable blank-body UX. Feature contracts orchestrate existing authorities and expose named evidence debt; they do not generate Flutter or React behavior, business algorithms, security rules, network schemas, or migrations.
